title: ulmus campestris f. variegata
intro: Ulmus campestris f. variegata, also known as the field elm or wych elm, is a variant of the common elm tree that exhibits variegated or marbled foliage. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ulmus campestris f. variegata include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
